title: Nanopore Sequencing of Environmental Microbial Metagenomes and Metatranscriptomes Using the Native Barcoding Kit 24 V14
author: Thuc Trong Nguyen

steps_raw.instruction: In a ZR BashingBead Lysis Tube (0.1 & 0.5 mm) , add up to 250 mg of Sample sample and 750 µL, DNA/RNA Shield . Ensuring a total volume of approximately 1000 µL per tube. 1000 µL

Close the tube caps tightly. Homogenize the sample using a Homogenizer Bead Mill or FastPrep-24 at a speed of 6 m/s for two rounds, each lasting 1 minute. Allow the sample tubes to cool On ice for 5 minutes between rounds.
Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds
Carefully transfer the entire supernatant into a 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, ensuring that the pellet remains undisturbed.
Add an equal volume of DNA/RNA Lysis Buffer to the supernatant at a 1:1 ratio (e.g., 400 μL of Lysis Buffer for 400 μL of sample). Mix thoroughly by vortexing, then briefly spin down.

Transfer the sample into a Spin-Away Filter (yellow) placed in a Collection Tube . Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Carefully transfer the flow-through to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube for RNA collection, and retain the filter for DNA purification.
Add an equal volume of absolute ethanol to the flow-through and mix thoroughly by pipetting.

Transfer the mix into a Zymo-Spin IIICG Column (green) placed in a Collection Tube . Centrifuge at 16000 rpm, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through. If necessary, reload the column and repeat the centrifugation.
Repeat the previous step until the entire mixture from Step 5 has been filtered.
DNase I Treatment in-column Add 400 µL, DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through

Prepare the following mix in a clean Eppendorf tube, adjusting the quantities based on the number of columns you use. Reaction mix Volume 1 DNase I reconstituted (1U/uL) 5 μL 2 DNA digestion buffer 75 μL 3 Total volume per reaction 80 μL Mix by gentle inversion, then briefly spin down. Do not vortex .
Add 80 µL, DNase I reaction Mix (from step 9.2) directly into the column matrix. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00
Add 400 µL, DNA/RNA Prep Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 16000 rpm, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through
Add 700 µL, DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 16000 rpm, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through
Add 400 µL, DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Carefully transfer the column into a new nuclease-free Eppendorf tube.

Add 100 µL, ZymoBIOMIC DNase/Rnase-Free Water directly to the column matrix. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 . Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds to elute RNA from the column. Alternatively, for highly concentrated RNA, use ≥50 mL elution
Place a Zymo-Spin III-HRC Filter in a Collection Tube. Add 600 µL, ZymoBIOMICS HRC Prep Solution . Centrifuge at 8000 x g, °C, 3 minutes .

Transfer the eluted RNA from Step 13 into a prepared Zymo-Spin III-HRC Filter ( Step 14 ) placed in a nuclease-free Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 3 minutes . The filtered RNA can be used immediately or stored frozen at -80 °C . Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit RNA HS).
Thoroughly shake the HighPrep RNA Elit e reagent before use. Allow the HighPrep RNA Elite reagent to reach Room temperature for at least 00:30:00 before use.
Vortex thoroughly and briefly spin down.
Transfer the total RNA sample into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Add the appropriate volume of HighPrep RNA Elite reagent based on the volume of your total RNA sample. %s %s
Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 6-8 times.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Place the mixture on a magnetic separation device for 00:15:00 or until the solution becomes clear.
While keeping the Eppendorf tube on the magnet , carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant.
With the tube on the magnet , add 200 µL, 70% ethanol to the Eppendorf tube. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30
While keeping the Eppendorf tube on the magnet , carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to settle again before removing the ethanol.
Repeat steps 23-24 for three 70% ethanol washes .

Add the following reagent to a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube Reagent Volume 1 Hybridization Buffer (HB) 5 μL 2 Resuspended RP 1 μL 3 RNA sample (containing 100ng - 5 ug of total RNA) 14 μL 4 RNaseOUT™ 0.5 μL 5 Total 20.5 μL

Dry the beads by incubating the Eppendorf tube on the magnetic separation device at Room temperature for up to 00:10:00 . Ensure that all traces of ethanol are completely removed. However, avoid overdrying the beads, as a cracked dry pellet may reduce the yield.

Remove the tube from the magnetic separation device. Add 40 µL, elution buffer (e.g., nuclease-free water) to the reaction tube and mix by pipetting up and down. Prewarming the nuclease-free water to 55 °C can help increase the yield.
Incubate the tube on a Hula mixer for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Briefly spin down the tube. Place it back on the magnetic separation device and wait for 00:03:00 or until the beads have completely cleared from the solution.
Transfer the eluate (cleared supernatant) to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube for storage or subsequent applications. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit RNA HS).
Incubate at 68 °C for 00:10:00 to denature RNA.

Allow the sample to cool gradually from 68 °C to 37 °C for optimal hybridisation. To achieve this, turn off the heating block and allow the temperature to naturally decrease to 37°C. If using temperature-controlled ramping, cool down at a rate of 3°C per minute.
Resuspend the streptavidin-coated magnetic beads (SMB) by carefully vortexingthe tube at medium speed.

Transfer 90 µL of bead suspension per sample into a fresh tube. For batch washing of beads for multiple samples, aliquot the bead suspension into a single tube: use up to 540 µL for 6 samples or 1080 µL for 12 samples.
Place the tube containing SMB beads on a magnetic rack and wait for 00:01:00 .
Remove and discard the supernatant carefully.
Add 80 µL of Depletion Buffer (DB) per sample (e.g., 480 µL for 6 samples and 960 µL for 12 samples). Agitate the tube well to fully resuspend the beads.
Repeat Steps 37-39 .
Briefly centrifuge the tube containing 20.5 µL of hybridized riboPOOL and total RNA (from Step 34 ).
Pipette 80 µL of the prepared beads (from Step 40 ) into the tube containing hybridised riboPOOL-RNA solution. Agitate the tube thoroughly to ensure proper resuspension.
Incubate the tube at 37 °C for 00:15:00 , followed by 50 °C for 00:05:00 in a thermal cycler.
Briefly spin down to collect any droplets.
Place on the magnet for 00:02:00 . Then, carefully transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.

Place the tube on the magnet for 00:01:00 to remove any trace amounts of beads (optional).Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new tube. At this stage, the elution should primarily contain mRNA . The RNA concentration is expected to decrease by approximately 90% from the initial amount due to the depletion of rRNA from the total RNA sample.

go to step #16 Follow the same protocol to purify total RNA precisely (from Step 16 to Step 30 ). The volume of elution buffer ( Step 27 ) can be adjusted to 30 µL to achieve a higher RNA concentration. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted RNA using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit RNA HS) Qualify 1 µL of the eluted RNA using either Bioanalyzer RNA 6000 Pico Kit (Agilent) or Qubit fluorometer (Qubit RNA IQ)

In a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, set up a 3’ polyadenylation reaction as follows Reagent Volume 1 Non-polyadenylated RNA (from step 47) X μL (e.g., 10 μL) 2 10X E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase Reaction Buffer 2 μL 3 ATP (10mM) 2 μL 4 E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase 1 μL 5 Nuclease-free water 14.5 μL - X μL (e.g., 14.5 - 10 = 4.5 μL) 6 RNaseOUT™ 0.5 μL 7 Total 20 μL Gently agitate by pipetting up and down. Do not vortex .
Incubate reaction at 37 °C for 00:10:00 .
Stop the reaction by adding 5 µL of 50 millimolar (mM), EDTA (to achieve a final concentration of 10 millimolar (mM), EDTA ). The final reaction volume will be 25 µL .
Add 45 µL of resuspended HighPrep RNA Elite Beads to the reaction tube.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device. Keep the tube on the magnet , carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant.

Keep the tube on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 70% ethanol. Gently rotate the tube 180° twice in the rack to ensure thorough washing of the pelleted beads. Carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant.
Repeat Step 54 to perform a total of two washes.
Briefly spin down the tube and place it back on the magnet . Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 12 µL of nuclease-free water. Incubate On ice for 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on the magnet until the beads pellet and the eluate becomes clear and colourless.
Carefully remove and retain 12 µL of the eluate containing the 3′-polyadenylated RNA in a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit RNA HS)
Prepare the following reaction in the 0.2 mL PCR tube: Reagent Volume 1 RNA (100 ng Poly(A)+ RNA) 7.5 μL 2 VN Primer diluted to 2 uM 2.5 μL 3 10 mM dNTPs 1 μL 4 Total 11 μL
In a separate tube, mix the following: Reagent Volume 1 5x RT Buffer 4 μL 2 RNase OUT 1 μL 3 Nuclease-free water 1 μL 4 Strand-switching Primer diluted to 10 uL 2 μL 5 Total 8 μL
Mix gently by flicking the tube, then briefly spin down.
Incubate at 65 °C for 00:05:00 and then snap cool on a pre-chilled freezer block for 00:01:00
Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down.
Incubate at 42 °C for 00:02:00 in the thermal cycler.
Add 8 µL of the strand-switching reagents (prepared in Step 63 ) to the 11 µL of snap-cooled mRNA (from Step 62 ). Mix gently by flicking the tube, then briefly spin down.
Add 1 µL of Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase , bringing the total volume to 20 µL .
Mix gently by flicking the tube, and spin down.

Incubate using the following protocol using a thermal cycler: Cycle step Temperature Time No. of cycles 1 Reverse transcription and strand- switching 42°C 90 mins 1 2 Heat inactivation 85°C 5 mins 1 3 Hold 4°C ∞
Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at 37 °C in the thermal cycler.
Add 1 µL of RNase Cocktail Enzyme Mix to the reverse transcription reaction (end product from Step 68 ).
Resuspend the AMPure XP beads (AXP) by vortexing thoroughly.
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device until the supernatant is clear and colourless. Keep the tube on the magnetic device and carefully pipette off the supernatant.
Transfer the sample to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Add 17 µL of resuspended AMPure XP beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix gently by flicking the tube.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device until the supernatant is clear and colourless. Keep the tube on the magnetic device and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Keep the tubes on the magnetic device and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol, ensuring the pellet remains undisturbed. Carefully remove and discard the ethanol using a pipette. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to re-pellet before removing the ethanol.
Repeat Step 76 for a total of two washes.

Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic device. Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for approximately 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 20 µL nuclease-free water.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
Briefly spin down the tube, then pellet the beads on the magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 00:01:00
Carefully remove and retain 20 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

Prepare the following reaction in a 0.2 mL thin-walled PCR tube: Reagent Volume 1 2x LongAmp Taq Master Mix 25 μl 2 PR2 Primer diluted to 10 μM 2 μl 3 Reverse-transcribed sample from above (Step 82) 20 μl 4 Nuclease-free water 3 μl 5 Total 50 μl Mix gently by flicking the tube
Incubate using the following protocol in a thermal cycler: Cycle step Temperature Time No. of cycles 1 Denaturation 94 °C 1 min 1 2 Annealing 50 °C 1 min 1 3 Extension 65 °C 15 mins 1 4 Hold 4 °C ∞
Resuspend the AMPure XP beads (AXP) by vortexing thoroughly.
Transfer the sample (end product of Step 84 ) to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Add 40 µL of resuspended AMPure XP beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix gently by flicking the tube.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat Step 90 for a total of two washes.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 21 µL nuclease-free water.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Briefly spin down the tube, then pellet the beads on the magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 00:01:00 .
Carefully remove and retain 21 µL of eluate in a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS)
Using a thermal cycler, incubate at 20 °C for 00:05:00 , followed by 65 °C for 00:05:00 .

Combine the following reagents in a 0.2 m PCR tube: Reagent Volume 1 cDNA sample (Step 96) 20 μl 2 Nuclease-free water 30 μl 3 Ultra II End-prep reaction buffer 7 μl 4 Ultra II End-prep enzyme mix 3 μl 5 Total 60 μl Ensure the reaction is thoroughly mixed by gently pipetting up and down, then briefly spin down.
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing thoroughly.
Transfer the sample ( Step 98 ) to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Add 60 µL of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the end-prep reaction ( Step 100 ) and mix gently by flicking the tube.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat Step 104 for a total of two washes
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 20 µL nuclease-free water. Incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature.
Pellet the beads on a magnetic rack until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 00:01:00

Remove and retain 20 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS) At this point, cDNA can be stored at -20 °C for up to a month or at -80 °C for longer storage.
For each sample to be run together on the same flow cell, select a unique barcode from the Native Barcodes (NB01-24) .

In clean 0.2 mL PCR tubes, add the reagents in the following order per tube: Reagent Volume 1 End-prepped DNA (from Step 109) 7.5 μl 2 Native Barcode (NB01-24) 2.5 μl 3 Blunt/TA Ligase Master Mix 10 μl 4 Total 20 μl Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting up and down, then briefly spin down.
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing.
Incubate for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .

Add the appropriate volume of EDTA to each tube, selecting the correct version based on the bottle cap colour. Mix thoroughly by pipetting, then briefly spin down. EDTA cap colour Volume per tube 1 For clear cap EDTA 2 μl 2 For blue cap EDTA 4 μl EDTA is added at this step to stop the reaction. The concentration of EDTA may vary depending on the version of your Nanopore reaction kit, as detailed below. The EDTA vials with a clear cap contain 0.5M EDTA (old version concentration). The EDTA vials with a blue cap contain 0.25M EDTA (new version concentration). The new EDTA concentration is indicated in all batch numbers formatted as NBD1424. 20 .0001 and NBD1424. 20 .0002 (or higher). The old format was used in batch numbers following the format NBD1424. 10 .0001.

Add AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the pooled reaction at a 0.4X ratio and mix thoroughly by pipetting. - Volume per sample For 6 samples For 12 samples For 24 samples 1 Volume of AXP for preps using clear cap EDTA 9 μl 53 μl 106 μl 211 μl 2 Volume of AXP for preps using blue cap EDTA 10 μl 58 μl 115 μl 230 μl
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 35 µL nuclease-free water by gently flicking.
Incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C . Every 00:02:00 , gently flick the sample for 00:00:10 to promote DNA elution.
Pellet the beads on a magnetic rack until the eluate is clear and colourless.
Carefully remove and retain 35 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS)

Thaw all reagents at Room temperature . Mix gently by pipetting, then briefly spin down. Place all reagents On ice . In a 1.5 ml Eppendorf LoBind tube, mix in the following order: Reagent Volume 1 Pooled barcoded sample 30 µL 2 Native Adapter (NA) 5 µL 3 NEBNext Quick Ligation Reaction Buffer (5X) 10 µL 4 Quick T4 DNA Ligase 5 µL 5 Total 50 µL IMPORTANT The Native Adapter (NA) used in this kit and protocol is not interchangeable with other sequencing adapters. Do NOT vortex the Quick T4 DNA Ligase .
Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting up and down, then briefly spin down.
Incubate the reaction for 00:20:00 at Room temperature
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing.
Add 20 µL of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix thoroughly by pipetting.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
Spin down the sample, then pellet it on the magnetic rack. Keep the tube on the magnet and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Wash the beads by adding 125 µL Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) . Flick the tube to resuspend the beads, then briefly spin down. Return the tube to the magnetic rack and allow the beads to pellet. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant using a pipette. IMPORTANT: Use Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) rather than 80% ethanol to wash the beads, as ethanol can be detrimental to the sequencing reaction.
Repeat Step 133 for a total of two washes.
Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic rack . Carefully pipette off any residual supernatant.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µL of Elution Buffer (EB) .
Spin down the tube and incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C . Every 00:02:00 , gently flick the sample for 00:00:10 to promote DNA elution.
Pellet the beads on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 00:01:00

Carefully remove and retain 15 µL of the eluate containing the DNA library in a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS) The prepared library is now ready for loading onto the flow cell. Store the library on ice until it is ready to be loaded.

In a 1.5 mL Eppendorf LoBind tube, mix in the following order: Reagent Volume per flow cell 1 Flow cell flush (FCF) 1170 μL 2 Bovine serum albumin (BSA) at 50 mg/mL 5 μL 3 Flow cell tether (FCT) 30 μL 4 Total 1205 μL Mix by pipetting at room temperature
Place the flow cell on the sequencing device and follow the manufacturer's instructions for setup and operation. MinION Flow Cell
Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port.

After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Drawback a small volume to remove any bubbles by following these sub-steps Visually check for a continuous buffer from the priming port across the sensor array.
Set a P1000 pipette to 200µL
Insert the tip into the priming port

Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µL , to draw back 20-30 µL or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip. IMPORTANT Take care when drawing back thebuffer from the flow cell. Do not remove more than 20-30 μl , and make sure that the array of pores is covered by buffer at all times. Introducing air bubbles into the array can irreversibly damage pores.

Load 800 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding introducing air bubbles. Wait for 00:05:00 . During this time, prepare the library for loading by following the steps below.
Thoroughly mix the contents of the Library Beads (LIB) by pipetting.

In a new 1.5 m Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: Reagent Volume per flow cell 1 Sequencing Buffer (SB) 37.5 μL 2 Library Beads (LIB) mixed immediately before use 25.5 μL 3 DNA library 12 μL 4 Total 75 μL
Complete the flow cell priming:
Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible.
Load 200 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port (not the SpotON sample port), avoiding introducing air bubbles.
Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just before loading.
Add 75 µL of the prepared library to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion . Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next.

Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover , ensuring the bung enters the SpotON port. Close the priming port. Place the light shield onto the flow cell. Close the device lid and set up a sequencing run. Start sequencing.

Keep the tubes on the magnetic device and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol, ensuring the pellet remains undisturbed. Carefully remove and discard the ethanol using a pipette. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to re-pellet before removing the ethanol.

Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic device . Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for approximately 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device until the supernatant is clear and colourless. Keep the tube on the magnetic device and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Keep the tubes on the magnetic device and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol, ensuring the pellet remains undisturbed. Carefully remove and discard the ethanol using a pipette. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to re-pellet before removing the ethanol.

Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic device . Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for approximately 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.

Pool all the barcoded samples in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. %s %s IMPORTANT: Ensure that the liquid volumes at the base of all tubes or wells in the plate are consistent before and after pooling to confirm that all liquid has been transferred correctly.
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device until the supernatant is clear and colourless. Keep the tube on the magnetic device and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Keep the tubes on the magnetic device and wash the beads with 700 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol, ensuring the pellet remains undisturbed. Carefully remove and discard the ethanol using a pipette. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to re-pellet before removing the ethanol.

Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic device . Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for approximately 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.
Repeat Step 119 for a total of two washes
Complete a flow cell check to assess the number of pores available before loading the library.

Combine the following reagents in a 0.2 m PCR tube (between each addition, pipette mix 10-20 times): Reagent Volume 1 DNA (from Step 29) 11 µL 2 DNA CS (optional) 1 µL 3 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Buffer 0.875 µL 4 NEBNext FFPE DNA Repair Mix 0.5 µL 5 Ultra II End-prep Reaction Buffer 0.875 µL 6 Ultra II End-prep Enzyme Mix 0.75 µL 7 Total 15 µL Ensure the reaction is thoroughly mixed by gently pipetting up and down, then briefly spin down.
Transfer the Spin-Away Filter ( yellow ) into a new Collection Tube .
Add 400 µL, DNA/RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through.
Add 700 µL, DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through.
Add 400 µL, DNA/RNA Wash Buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Carefully transfer the column into a new nuclease-free Eppendorf tube.

Add 100 µL, ZymoBIOMIC DNase/Rnase-Free Water directly to the column matrix. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 . Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 30 seconds to elute RNA from the column. Alternatively, for highly concentrated RNA, use ≥50 mL elution
Place a Zymo-Spin III-HRC Filter in a Collection Tube. Add 600 µL, ZymoBIOMICS HRC Prep Solution . Centrifuge at 8000 x g, °C, 3 minutes .

Transfer the eluted RNA from Step 12 into a prepared Zymo-Spin III-HRC Filter ( Step 13 ) placed in a nuclease-free Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 3 minutes . The filtered RNA can be used immediately or stored frozen at -80 °C . Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS).
Thoroughly shake the AMPure XP reagent before use. Allow the AMPure XP reagent to reach Room temperature for at least 00:30:00 before use.
Transfer the DNA sample into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.
Add the appropriate volume of AMPure XP reagent based on the volume of your total DNA sample. %s %s
Mix thoroughly by pipetting up and down 8-10 times.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Place the mixture on a magnetic separation device for 00:05:00 or until the solution becomes clear.
While keeping the Eppendorf tube on the magnet , carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant.
With the tube on the magnet , add 200 µL, 70% ethanol to the Eppendorf tube. Incubate at Room temperature for 00:00:30
While keeping the Eppendorf tube on the magnet , carefully pipette off and discard the supernatant. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to settle again before removing the ethanol.
Repeat Steps 22-23 for two 70% ethanol washes .

Dry the beads by incubating the Eppendorf tube on the magnetic separation device at Room temperature for up to 00:05:00 . Ensure that all traces of ethanol are completely removed. However, avoid overdrying the beads, as a cracked dry pellet may reduce the yield.

Remove the tube from the magnetic separation device. Add 40 µL, elution buffer (e.g., nuclease-free water) to the reaction tube and mix by pipetting up and down. Prewarming the nuclease-free water to 55 °C can help increase the yield.
Incubate the tube on a Hula mixer for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Briefly spin down the tube. Place it back on the magnetic separation device and wait for 00:03:00 or until the beads have completely cleared from the solution.

Transfer the eluate (cleared supernatant) to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube for storage or subsequent applications. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS). Purified DNA can be stored in -80 °C freezer for up to a year.
Transfer the End-prepped DNA into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat Step 37 for two 80% ethanol washes .
Using a thermal cycler, incubate at 20 °C for 00:05:00 , followed by 65 °C for 00:05:00 .
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing thoroughly.
Add 15 µL of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the end-prep reaction ( Step 33 ) and mix gently by flicking the tube.
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device until the supernatant is clear and colourless. Keep the tube on the magnetic device and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Keep the tubes on the magnetic device and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol, ensuring the pellet remains undisturbed. Carefully remove and discard the ethanol using a pipette. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to re-pellet before removing the ethanol.

Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic device . Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for approximately 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 10 µL nuclease-free water. Incubate for 00:02:00 at room temperature.
Pellet the beads on a magnetic rack until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 00:01:00

Remove and retain 10 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS) DNA can be stored at -20 °C for up to a month or at -80 °C for longer storage.
For each sample to be run together on the same flow cell, select a unique barcode from the Native Barcodes (NB01-24) .

In clean 0.2 mL PCR tubes, add the reagents in the following order per tube: Reagent Volume 1 End-prepped DNA (from Step 42) 7.5 μl 2 Native Barcode (NB01-24) 2.5 μl 3 Blunt/TA Ligase Master Mix 10 μl 4 Total 20 μl Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting up and down, then briefly spin down.
Incubate for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .

Add the appropriate volume of EDTA to each tube, selecting the correct version based on the bottle cap colour. Mix thoroughly by pipetting, then briefly spin down. EDTA cap colour Volume per tube 1 For clear cap EDTA 2 μl 2 For blue cap EDTA 4 μl EDTA is added at this step to stop the reaction. The concentration of EDTA may vary depending on the version of your Nanopore reaction kit, as detailed below. The EDTA vials with a clear cap contain 0.5M EDTA (old version concentration). The EDTA vials with a blue cap contain 0.25M EDTA (new version concentration). The new EDTA concentration is indicated in all batch numbers formatted as NBD1424. 20 .0001 and NBD1424. 20 .0002 (or higher). The old format was used in batch numbers following the format NBD1424. 10 .0001.

Pool all the barcoded samples in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. %s %s IMPORTANT: Ensure that the liquid volumes at the base of all tubes or wells in the plate are consistent before and after pooling to confirm that all liquid has been transferred correctly.
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing.

Add AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the pooled reaction at a 0.4X ratio and mix thoroughly by pipetting. - Volume per sample For 6 samples For 12 samples For 24 samples 1 Volume of AXP for preps using clear cap EDTA 9 μl 53 μl 106 μl 211 μl 2 Volume of AXP for preps using blue cap EDTA 10 μl 58 μl 115 μl 230 μl
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
Spin down the sample, then pellet it using a magnetic separation device until the supernatant is clear and colourless. Keep the tube on the magnetic device and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Keep the tubes on the magnetic device and wash the beads with 700 µL of freshly prepared 80% ethanol, ensuring the pellet remains undisturbed. Carefully remove and discard the ethanol using a pipette. If the pellet is disturbed, allow the beads to re-pellet before removing the ethanol.
Repeat Step 52 for a total of two washes .

Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic device . Carefully pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow the beads to dry for approximately 00:00:30 , ensuring the pellet does not over-dry or crack.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 35 µL nuclease-free water by gently flicking.
Incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C . Every 00:02:00 , gently flick the sample for 00:00:10 to promote DNA elution.
Pellet the beads on a magnetic rack until the eluate is clear and colourless.
Carefully remove and retain 35 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS).

Thaw all reagents at Room temperature . Mix gently by pipetting, then briefly spin down. Place all reagents On ice . In a 1.5 mL Eppendorf LoBind tube, mix in the following order (Between each addition, gently mix by pipetting up and down 10-20 times): Reagent Volume 1 Pooled barcoded sample 30 µL 2 Native Adapter (NA) 5 µL 3 NEBNext Quick Ligation Reaction Buffer (5X) 10 µL 4 Quick T4 DNA Ligase 5 µL 5 Total 50 µL IMPORTANT The Native Adapter (NA) used in this kit and protocol is not interchangeable with other sequencing adapters. Do NOT vortex the Quick T4 DNA Ligase .
Thoroughly mix the reaction by gently pipetting up and down, then briefly spin down.
Incubate the reaction for 00:20:00 at Room temperature
Resuspend the AMPure XP Beads (AXP) by vortexing.
Add 20 µL of resuspended AMPure XP Beads (AXP) to the reaction and mix thoroughly by pipetting.
Incubate on a Hula mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
Spin down the sample, then pellet it on the magnetic rack. Keep the tube on the magnet and carefully pipette off the supernatant.

Wash the beads by adding either 125 µL of Long Fragment Buffer (LFB) or Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) . Flick the tube to resuspend the beads, then briefly spin down. Return the tube to the magnetic rack and allow the beads to pellet. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant using a pipette. IMPORTANT : Use either Long Fragment Buffer (LFB) or Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) rather than 80% ethanol to wash the beads, as ethanol can be detrimental to the sequencing reaction. The clean-up step after adapter ligation is designed to selectively enrich DNA fragments based on the wash buffer used: To enrich for DNA fragments ≥3 kb, use Long Fragment Buffer (LFB) . To retain DNA fragments of all sizes, use Short Fragment Buffer (SFB) .
Repeat Step 66 for a total of two washes .
Spin down the tube and place it back on the magnetic rack . Carefully pipette off any residual supernatant.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend the pellet in 15 µL of Elution Buffer (EB) .
Spin down the tube and incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C . Every 00:02:00 , gently flick the sample for 00:00:10 to promote DNA elution.
Pellet the beads on a magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless, for at least 00:01:00

Carefully remove and retain 15 µL of the eluate containing the DNA library in a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube. Quantify 1 µL of the eluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer (Qubit DNA HS) The prepared library is now ready for loading onto the flow cell. Store the library on ice until it is ready to be loaded.

In a 1.5 mL Eppendorf LoBind tube, mix in the following order: Reagent Volume per flow cell 1 Flow cell flush (FCF) 1170 μL 2 Bovine serum albumin (BSA) at 50 mg/mL 5 μL 3 Flow cell tether (FCT) 30 μL 4 Total 1205 μL Mix by pipetting at room temperature
Place the flow cell on the sequencing device and follow the manufacturer's instructions for setup and operation. MinION Flow Cell
Complete a flow cell check to assess the number of pores available before loading the library.
Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port.

After opening the priming port, check for a small air bubble under the cover. Drawback a small volume to remove any bubbles by following these sub-steps Visually check for a continuous buffer from the priming port across the sensor array.
Set a P1000 pipette to 200µL
Insert the tip into the priming port

Turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230 µL , to draw back 20-30 µL or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip. IMPORTANT Take care when drawing back thebuffer from the flow cell. Do not remove more than 20-30 μl , and make sure that the array of pores is covered by buffer at all times. Introducing air bubbles into the array can irreversibly damage pores.

Load 800 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding introducing air bubbles. Wait for 00:05:00 . During this time, prepare the library for loading by following the steps below.
Thoroughly mix the contents of the Library Beads (LIB) by pipetting.

In a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows: Reagent Volume per flow cell 1 Sequencing Buffer (SB) 37.5 μL 2 Library Beads (LIB) mixed immediately before use 25.5 μL 3 DNA library 12 μL 4 Total 75 μL
Complete the flow cell priming:
Gently lift the SpotON sample port cover to make the SpotON sample port accessible.
Load 200 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port (not the SpotON sample port), avoiding introducing air bubbles.
Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting up and down just before loading.
Add 75 µL of the prepared library to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion . Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next.

Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover , ensuring the bung enters the SpotON port. Close the priming port . Place the light shield onto the flow cell. Close the device lid and set up a sequencing run. Start sequencing.
